info.name: Amrod's Client Vendors 2.0.11
info.description:
This documentation provides Amrod's customers with detailed information on the API endpoints that are available for use to integrate into our solutions and our data. To **gain access** to these API's please complete the following form [https://content.amrod.co.za/new-api-access-request](https://content.amrod.co.za/new-api-access-request). For more information about the API's please visit this url: [https://content.amrod.co.za/new-api-resources
](https://content.amrod.co.za/new-api-resources) If you have any further access issues once your credentials are provided, please email [api.support@amrod.co.za](https://mailto:api.support@amrod.co.za). # Important URLS **Authentication URL** [https://identity.amrod.co.za](https://identity.amrod.co.za) **Authentication Get Token URL** [https://identity.amrod.co.za/VendorLogin](https://identity.amrod.co.za/VendorLogin) **Vendor API URL** [https://vendorapi.amrod.co.za](https://vendorapi.amrod.co.za) ## "The API is not working in my browser" **You cannot access the vendor API by visiting the url in a web browser** The API does not allow you to access the information by visiting it in a browser. It needs to be connected via a IDE, backend code or postman. Please see [https://forum.amrod.co.za/topic/37/can-i-get-a-token-by-visiting-the-url-in-my-browser](https://forum.amrod.co.za/topic/37/can-i-get-a-token-by-visiting-the-url-in-my-browser) for more info. # Ver 2.0.11 - Pricing and Stock Mapping Details ## Notice Date: 2026-02 We have elaborated on the mapping / matching of Stock and Pricing Endpoints to further explain that this is done on a Products' Variant Full Code. Please see appropriate sections for Stock and Pricing for more information. # Ver 2.0.10 - Summary ## Deployment Date: 2025-11 A new Colour Group endpoint has been created. The purpose of this is to provide a grouping of similar colours e.g. A group called "Blue" would include Cyan, Light Blue and Dark Blue. The previous Colour Swatch endpoint has been updated as the IsDeleted field / flag has been deprecated and has been removed. # Ver 2.0.9 - Summary ## Deployment Date: 2025-09 Category endpoint has been updated to include Unique ID that identifies each Nested Category. This ID is also added in the product endpoints so that you can easily link the product to the correct category. There are changes within the Product Module and Category Module. Please view those sections for more information. # Ver 2.0.8 - Summary ## Deployment Date: 2025-02 Inclusive Branding Specials have been upgraded to include a position to which that special applies to. # Ver 2.0.7 - Summary ## Deployment Date: 2024-10 Colour Swatch endpoint has been added to allow one to view the actual colour # / HEX(s) for all our products. # Ver 2.0.6 - Summary ## Deployment Date: 2024-08 _**!!! Important API Availabily Time Change**_ 1. The API's will not be available between the times of 00:00 and 01:00 2. These times are GMT+2 3. Any calls made to the API including products, pricing and stock will return a **204 HTTP Response** 1. This means it will return a success but with no content returned 4. This has been implemented to ensure that all content is sync, manipulated and prepared to be correct for all users to consume # Ver 2.0.5 - Summary ## Deployment Date: 2024-02 1. Includes additional insights for Authentication challenges 1. Includes 3 new return values if login not successful 2. Includes common mistakes in inputting the values 2. Included information on Inclusive Branding 1. Outlined the 4 different Branding Flags # Ver 2.0.4 - Summary ## Deployment Date: 2023-10 1. Includes additional info for promotional flags on Products 2. Single Sign-on and API and Website Access 3. Determine if a record is NEW, UPDATED or to be DELETED in Get Updated End Point # Ver 2.0.3 - Summary ## Deployed Date: 2023-06-20 Includes minor updates based on internal review + further elaboration on unclear areas. # Ver 2.0.2 - Summary ## Deployed Date: 2023-06-09 Documentation review and updates. # Ver 2.0.1 - Summary ## Deployed Date: 2023-06-01 All modules / endpoints are provided with 2 distinct options: 1. Get All 2. Get Changes ## Get All The **Get All** endpoints can be found in each module and returns the full dataset of all the information within that specific module. Data found in the **Get All** endpoints are updated once a day therefore if you have called this endpoint already there would be no need to call it within the next 24hrs as information you will receive would be the same as the prior call. ## Get Changes The **Get** **Changes** endpoints can be found in each module and returns only the last changes that have been made within the last 24hrs for a specific module. Data found in the **Get Changes** endpoints are updated once a day and **only** contain the changes that have been made within the last 24hr period therefore if you have called this endpoint already there would be no need to call it within the next 24hrs as information you will receive would be the same as the prior call. As these endpoints only return changes for this period it means that changes older than 24hrs (2 days) are not available. If you do not utilise this endpoint to keep your data updated daily OR miss a day you would need to call the **Get All** endpoint to get the latest copy of all data. # Product Structure Each product is made up of 2 elements - Variant - Base ## Variant The variant is the actual item that is sold. It generally comes in a colour or a size and each variant is a combination of the 2 e.g. If a t-shirt is sold in 3 colours with each having 4 sizes, then there are 12 variants (4 x 3). ## Base The Base is the default information about a product. It contains important information such as branding information that is common to all variants. It isn't something that one can buy e.g. If a t-shirt is sold in 3 colours with each having 4 sizes, one cannot order a t-shirt by itself as it is not a sellable product, you would have to define the size and colour first. # Codes (SKU) We have 2 types of codes in our system. - Simple - Full ## Simple The simple code is the code of the "Base" item as defined above. It is used for information common to all of the products in that set and does not include any size or colour information. ## Full The full code is the code of the "Variant" item as defined above. It is used to define the exact size and or colour of the item you are looking to order. # Branding Options The Product Module is split into 2 distinct endpoints: 1. With Branding 2. Without Branding The purpose is to allow each customer the option of what information they require for use within their business and systems whilst keeping the information optimized and easy to use. ## Branding Positions Positions are included in the product schema. They are provided at the a **Base level** and applies to each variant. Each Position includes 1 or many branding methods available for that position. As not all methods are available for all colours, each method (within a position) includes a field for the "excluded" colours e.g. Sublimation is only offered on white items and therefore this method would include the remaining colours in the "Excluded" field. This list of excluded values is a comma separated list of colour codes and means that any of the variants of that colour will all be unable to brand that method in that position. ## Branding Pricing Each Branding Code has a few pricing options. All products with the same Branding Codes will have the same pricing structure and within that Branding Code you would use a combination of the Branding Code, QTY ordered and the colour options selected to get the correct pricing for a specific order. **Note:** Because of the above we recommend using this as a "look up" table to be synced daily only once in order to get all main values up to date. Your logic on check out / order placement would then select the appropriate matching pricing. ## Inclusive Branding Most products contain an Inclusive Branding Method/Option which has a combination of offerings from free branding of a branding code to multiple positions etc. Unlike Branding Pricing, each Inclusive Branding is per product and at the Branding Position level. These Inclusive Branding Method/Options are in addition to the standard Branding Positions and methods outlined above. E.g. If a product has Embroidery on Position A as well as a Free Embroidery on Position A it would have 2 options for a user to choose from. This is because you have multiple positions so Embroidery can be on Position A and B but only free on Position A. Inclusive Branding is not only specific to a product and its method but also specific to a position. This is because, in some instances, we have multiple positions that may have the same method however only the specific position is included as a special. The structure of this is provided the same as the previous Feed / XML / API. - Inclusive Brandings - This module is used to get Inclusive Branding information. - inclusive Branding Prices - This module is used to get Inclusive Branding Pricing information. Inclusive Brandings include a replacement code which indicates that if Inclusive Branding is used then the branding code needs to be replaced with a new one. - Branding Pricing - This module is used to get all Branding Prices per Branding Code. **Note:** All Branding related endpoints have been moved to a folder called "Branding". These endpoints are only required if you intend to utilise branding pricing and Inclusive Branding on your solution. These are master lookups and do not include differentials.

item.name: Auth

request.description:
The purpose of this endpoint is to authenticate the user and, if successful, provides an authentication token for that user to use in further API calls.

This is the first API call needed in order to access any other API endpoints.

Once a token has been provided you can use the same token for multiple calls. You do not need to get new tokens for each API call.

Tokens however do expire (1hr from generation of the token), therefore if you receive a 401 Unauthorised Status Code please refresh your token by re-authenticating it once again.

If there are multiple users requiring access, we would recommend that each user / system utilise their own credentials and not share them between users or integrations.

**Message returned instead of Valid Token**

If you are having issues getting a valid token and are sure the login details work on the website, please ensure the following items are set:

- Invalid User
    - The username you are using (Email) is not found
- Invalid username or password.
    - The username you are using (Email) was found but there the either the password or customer code is incorrect
- API Access Denied
    - The username you are using (Email) was found and they have a valid password and customer code combination HOWEVER this account has not been given API access

**Can't Get Valid Token**

If you are having issues getting a valid token and are sure the login details work on the website, please ensure the following items are set:

- Please Set Content-Type header
    - 'Content-Type: application/json',
- Please specify that you expect a JSON response it is optional
    - 'Accept: application/json'
- Check you are using strings in your VendorLogin
    - INCORRECT
        - "Username": "{{myemail@mycompany.com}}""
    - CORRECT
        - "Username": "[myemail@mycompany.com](https://mailto:myemail@mycompany.com)""

## Authentication URL

{{IdentityServerUrl}}/VendorLogin

item.description:
Tokens are required for all endpoints except for the login / auth endpoints.

Once you have a valid token, please use this as a "bearer" token for all future API/ endpoint calls.

A valid token can be used for multiple different APIcalls and will remain valid until it expires.

Tokens "Time to Live"(TTL) is set to 1hr from time of creation.

If you had a valid token and suddenly receive a 401 Unauthorised Status Code please refresh your token by re-authenticating it once again.

Usernames and Passwords will be provided by Amrod and if you have any access issues, please email [api.support@amrod.co.za](https://mailto:api.support@amrod.co.za).

## Important URLS

**Authentication BASE URL**

{{IdentityServerUrl}}

**Vendor API URL**

{{CatalogueVendorApiUrl}}

**Get Access Token URL**

{{CatalogueVendorApiUrl}}/VendorLogin

## "The API is not working in my browser"

**You cannot access the vendor API by visiting the url in a web browser**  
The API does not allow you to access the information by visiting it in a browser. It needs to be connected via a IDE, backend code or postman. Please see [https://forum.amrod.co.za/topic/37/can-i-get-a-token-by-visiting-the-url-in-my-browser](https://forum.amrod.co.za/topic/37/can-i-get-a-token-by-visiting-the-url-in-my-browser) for more info.

# Single Sign-On - API + Website User

Amrod has implemented a single sign-on solution. This means that the same credentials can access both the Website and the API.

If the API is using the same credentials as Website user it means that if the user resets their password on the website, it would mean the API credentials need to change as well.

**IMPORTANT NOTE:**

## Recommended

The 2 integration methodologies that we recommend are:

1. Full Initial Import
    
    1. The data returned is extremely fast therefore you would be abel to get the information daily if required
        
2. Once a week in order to get the information up to date
    
    1. If you aren't able to process the data once a day, you can get it once a week to keep your systems in line
        
    2. It is important to note that this will mean any changes made within the week on Amrods' side will not reflect on your side yet

item.name: Catalogue Vendor API
Products

request.description:
The purpose of this endpoint is to return all the products **without Branding**. This includes both products and Giftsets. The returned information includes a TYPE field that denotes if this is a product / giftset.

Giftsets have additional _components_ object that includes the "parts" that make up the gift set.

This endpoint **EXCLUDES** branding information. If you require product Branding, Positions, Methods and Inclusive Branding etc please use the "With Branding" endpoint

**NOTE:**  
It is recommended to only call the "With Branding" endpoint if you offer branding and are requiring this information for your website or internal systems. If you call the "With Branding" end point and you do not require branding it will still return information, however it would include fields and data you do not require therefore making the result set larger and your processing slower as it needs to work through data you are not needing.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these 2 requirements
    1. Full Initial Import
    2. Once a week in order to get items in sync
2. It can be used to call the information once a day, however if you are keeping the information in sync daily, we would recommend using the "Get Changes" endpoint as this will only return the changes from the previous day resulting in a smaller set of data to process
    

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Called multiple times within the day
    1. This is because products are only updating once a day therefore calling this endpoint will not result in any additional changes

The purpose of this endpoint is to returns <u>only </u> the products **without Branding** that have been <u>updated or changed</u> since the previous day. The purpose of this endpoint is to allow a user to keep their local copy of the product catalogue up to date regularly to avoid having to do the entire catalogue each day and rather only process the changed records.

This includes both products and Giftsets. The returned information includes a TYPE field that denotes if this is a product / giftset.

Giftsets have additional _components_ object that includes the "parts" that make up the gift set.

This endpoint **EXCLUDES** branding information. If you require product Branding, Positions, Methods or Inclusive Branding etc please use the "With Branding" endpoint.

An "Action" field is returned for each record and is the only field returned on these endpoints.

The field is called "ActionType" and it denotes the type of change. It is an integer field that correlates to the following types / status / actions:

- **0**
    - This means it has been created
- **1**
    - This means that record has changed or been updated
- **2**
    - This means this record has been removed

It is provided to advise you on what changes they include and what actions to implement.

**NOTE:**  
It is recommended to only call the "With Branding" endpoint if you offer branding and are requiring this information for your website or internal systems.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended to only be called for these 2 requirements
    1. Once a Get All is called to get All the latest information
    2. Once a day after the above step has been actioned

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Once a week
    1. This endpoint only returns information changed since the previous day
    2. Any changes made prior to that are removed and replaced with only the latest changes
    3. if you do not call this daily and or miss one or more days your data may be out of sync and not up to date
2. Called multiple times within the day
    1. This is because products are only updating once a day therefore calling this endpoint will not result in any additional changes

The purpose of this endpoint is to return all the products **with Branding**. This includes both products and Giftsets. The returned information includes a TYPE field that denotes if this is a product / giftset.

Giftsets have additional component objects that includes the "parts" that make up the gift set.

This end**point INCLUDES** branding information. If you require products without branding or do not require the branding information for your website or systems then please use the "Get All " endpoint

**NOTE:**  
It is recommended to only call the "With Branding" endpoint if you offer branding and are requiring this information for your website or internal systems.

## Branding Structure

The branding structure is provided in a tree view:

- Position
    - Methods

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these 2 requirements
    1. Full Initial Import
    2. Once a week in order to get items in sync
2. It can be used to call the information once a day, however if you are keeping the information in sync daily, we would recommend using the "Get Changes" endpoint as this will only return
    

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Called multiple times within the day
    1. This is because products are only updated once a day therefore calling this endpoint will not result in any additional changes

The purpose of this endpoint is to return <u>only</u> the products **with Branding** that have been <u>updated or changed</u> since the previous day. The purpose of this endpoint is to allow a user to keep their local copy of the product catalogue up to date regularly to avoid having to do the entire catalogue each day and rather only process the changed records.

This includes both products and Giftsets. The returned information includes a TYPE field that denotes if this is a product / giftset.

Giftsets have additional component objects that includes the "parts" that make up the gift set.

This endpoint **INCLUDES** branding information. If you require product Branding, Positions, Methods or Inclusive Branding etc please use the "With Branding" endpoint.

An "Action" field is returned for each record and is the only field returned on these endpoints.

The field is called "ActionType" and it denotes the type of change. It is an integer field that correlates to the following types / status / actions:

- **0**
    - This means it has been created
- **1**
    - This means that record has changed or been updated
- **2**
    - This means this record has been removed

It is provided to advise you on what changes they include and what actions to implement.

**NOTE:**  
It is recommended to only call the "With Branding" endpoint if you offer branding and are requiring this information for your website or internal systems.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for the following 2 requirements
    1. Once a Get All is called to get All the latest information
    2. Once a day after the above step has been actioned

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Once a week
    1. This endpoint only returns information changed since the previous day
    2. Any changes made prior to that are removed and replaced with only the latest changes
    3. if you do not call this daily and or miss one or more days your data may be out of sync and not up to date
2. Called multiple times within the day
    1. This is because products are only updated once a day therefore calling this endpoint will not result in any additional changes
3. No Branding Required
    1. If you do not require the branding information we would recommend using the "Without Branding" endpoints

item.description: This module is used to get Product specific information. This includes basic information as well as related content such as default product information, variants (size and colour), branding options, carton dimensions, product attributes, product images, branding templates and guides, related, companion and gender matched products. ## Update Frequency Amrod updates _Product_ information only once a day. # Product Structure Each product is made up of 2 elements - Base - Variant **NOTE:** Each product always has a base and at least one variant. All variants are linked / grouped under the same base. ## Base The Base is the default information about a product. It contains important information such as branding information that is common to all variants. It isn't something that one can buy E.g. If a t-shirt is sold in 3 colours with each having 4 sizes, one cannot order a t-shirt by itself as it isn't a sellable product, you would have to define the size and colour first. ## Variant The variant is the actual item that is sold. It generally comes in a colour and or a size as well and each variant is a combination of the 2 E.g. If a t-shirt is sold in 3 colours with each having 4 sizes, then there are 12 variants (4 x 3). ## Simple vs Full Codes / SKU's Each product contains a Simple and Full Code. The Simple Code is the base code of the product and the Full Code is the code of that specific Variant. - In the **Base product** information these 2 codes are the same. - In **each Variant product**, the Full Code includes the full Size and or Colour (if applicable). **NOTE:** Stock and Price information matches on the Products Variant Code and not on the Base. ## Decoupled Because each product variant is linked / grouped under the same base, in some instances we "decouple" the variant. This may be for numerous reasons (such as end of life or clearance or specials) but in essence it means that this specific colour is sold separately to the base. This can be seen by having the "decoupled" flag on that base set to true. It means that this specific base is no longer linked to the "grouped" base but is rather its own product and is sold separately. The grouped base will be updated to only show the remaining variants. All decoupled items are then moved to different products including colour images, pricing and branding options. An example is if you are selling a t-shirt that has 5 colours and 6 different sizes. Amrod may choose to offer the RED colour only on a special or discontinue that product. That would mean you would have a base of 4 colours and 1 decoupled product with only the RED colour sizes in it. In this example the Simple Code of the decoupled product's Simple Code would include the -R suffix. ## Categories Each product contains an object that outlines the categories that it belongs to. Categories are provided in a (parent/child) tree structure and products may appear in 1 or multiple categories. Each category object includes an ID - which is unique and maps the position on the category tree. The Path field outlines a high level view of the names of each structure level. The full relationship structure can be found in the "Get Categories" endpoints. ## Promotion and Visible Flags For each BASE product we provide additional flags that allow one to group or promote different products. **Behaviour** - 0 - **Normal** - 1 - **Featured** - 2 - **Hidden** **Promotion** - 0 - **Normal** - 1 - **On Promotion** - 2 - **New** - 3 - **Clearance** ## Product Dimensions, Packing Dimensions and Product Attributes Each variant would contain its own data relating to: - Product Dimensions - The sizes of **each individual** product - Split into - Length (in cm) - Width (in cm) - Weight (in cm) - Height (in cm) - Package Dimensions - Provides the package dimensions - Split into - Pieces per carton - Carton Dimensions - Length (in cm) - Width (in cm) - Height (in cm) - Carton Weight (in KG's) - Attributes - Describe the actual product variant - e.g. 1/2 Chest size - It is provided as a key value pair and has different keys based on the product - e.g. Top would have 1/2 Chest and Pants would have Length ## Components Only giftsets include components. Components are the products that are included and make up a giftset. Components includes a list of product codes as well as the individual branding position options that can be branded per component. If it is a product that has no components. This is defined in the Type field of each record. # Images ## Base vs Variant In each product there are 2 image nodes: - Images - This relates to the BASE images showcasing the entire product - colourImages - This relates to the VARIANT images grouped by colour In each image row we provide the URL as well as additional meta data describing the image e.g. - IsDefault - Boolean showing if it is the 1st image / default image or if it is an additional image showcasing that item - hasLogo - If the image contains an example of a branding logo This will be expanded to provide additional information for you to use to filter images. ## Image Sizes Images are included in the endpoints and contain the full URL / Path to each image. They are provided on **ONE SIZE ONLY** - that being 1024 x 1024 size image. Amrod provides each image in multiple different sizes. The URL provided has the string 1024x1024 in the actual URL and you can use this to get any other size required for your needs by simply replacing the size above with any of the provided sizes below. Here is a list of available image size (Width x Height) - in pixels: 1. 151X141 2. 460X350 3. 46X45 4. 260X250 5. 270X150 So to get the above items you can take the image URL and replace 1024x1024 with 151x141 (as an example). **Note**: - The sizes above do not have any spaces or underscores - The X between the height and width is UPPERCASE and is case sensitive - The sizes must be as above and must be used as provided e.g. 460x350 and not 350x460 - No full version size is provided in the API - All sizes outlined above are in px (Pixels) # Branding The product endpoints are provided with 2 options: 1. With Branding 2. Without Branding The split has been done to provide those without branding a smaller set of information and the users who require branding a more structured product result. The url to the Branding Guide is always provided no matter which option you choose as this guide provides a summary of the branding options, image of the product and clear branding areas and methods. Even when one does not provide branding directly on one's website it would still allow customers to download the guide to see options available. ## Branding Structure If you require branding we provide this in the following tree structure: - Positions - Methods - Exclusions **EXCLUSIONS NOTE**: It is important to note that the branding is provided at the **BASE** level meaning those methods are available for all **VARIANTS.** Because some methods may not be suitable on all colours an Exclusion list is provided with only the colour codes. This would mean that the method is available for all variants EXCEPT the ones within those excluded colour codes. If a colour code is provided it would exclude all variants (size options) within that colour. ### Positions Positions relate to where branding can be added on an item. The results include the position code and name of the position which correlates to the branding guide. A position multiplier means if a position can be branded multiple times e.g. If Position A has "Left or Right of chest" it means that you can brand that position in 2 places, Left and Right, so it would have a position multiplier of 2. It would result in Position Code A-1 and A-2 being created on an order / branding implementation. ### Methods Methods are contained within each position allowing one to know what types of branding are avalaible per position. This includes all the information required for branding using that method e.g. Max Print Size, Number of Colours and branding codes. ### Exclusions Within each method there is an Exclusion field. This is a comma separated list including the colour codes where this method is NOT allowed. If the exclusions are empty it means that both the method and position can be performed on ALL variants. ### RequiredBrandingPositions Some products have required branding positions which are required if you are doing a branding on that product. This field includes a comma separated list of the Position Codes. ### NoCoBrandingPositions Some products have a field called No Co Branding positions. No Co Branding means that there is a branding position that is required by Amrod however you cannot provide your own artwork for this position as the branding is fixed and cannot be changed e.g. Stick with a specific design, the no co branding means you cannot add any other logos or artwork to this position. This field includes a comma separated list of the Position Codes. # Linked Products Additional information is provided on each product relating to linked products. ## Companion This includes the list of product codes that go well with a specific product e.g. If you are ordering a notebook then a companion product may be a pen or if you are ordering a t-shirt the companion may be a pair of pants. ## Related This includes the list of product codes that are similar to a specific product e.g. If you are ordering a notebook then a related product may be a different notebook product. ## Matching This includes the list of product codes that match the product based on Gender e.g. if you are looking at a Ladies T-shirt the matching would indicate the code of the product that is the same but in for Men. ## Access You are required to have a valid token as well as authorization to call the endpoints in this module.

item.name: Stock

request.description:
The purpose of this endpoint is to return all the stock for all products 4 times a day (every 6 hours as outlined below).

This endpoint returns current and incoming stock.

Incoming stock may include multiple records for a specific product / variant and as such the ETA and Quantity for each order expected is included in the results of each endpoint.

## Update Frequency

Amrod updates the stock of All its products multiple times within a day:

- 06:30
    
- 12:30
    
- 18:30
    
- 00:30
    

Times are GMT+2

As the changes will have already been updated please all call the Get Updated endpoint after the Get All endpoint has finished to ensure you have the latest product stock levels.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these 2 requirements
    
    1. Once a day in order to get all stock in sync
        
    2. StartFragmentThree times a a day as per the schedule outlined above
        

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Called multiple times within the same schedule window
    
    1. As the All Stock endpoint is only updated as per the scheduled times above, calling it multiple times during each window will result in no updates being made and the same information returned that you would have previously received and processed
        
    2. If you want to keep the stock up to date, please call Get All once and then use the Get Changed endpoint to keep all stock information up to date
        

**IMPORTANT:**

Stock provided by these endpoints match on the product variant's **Full Code**. We do provide Stock for the Colour Groupings and Totals for the entire product set. This is outlined in the "Stock" introduction above.

The purpose of this endpoint is to return a rolling / differential of the stock change since the 1st Full Stock sync as per the timeline defined (00:30 GMT+2).

## Schedule

The full stock list is cleared and repopulated with the latest stock levels of all products at that time.

After this initial sync we are keeping a list of unique product stock that has changed since that time.

It will include a unique record per stock item therefore if the stock was run at 3am and there have been 10 changes since 3am and 2pm and this endpoint is called at 2pm it will return only 1 record for that product which is the stock as of 2pm.

This endpoint returns current and incoming stock.

Incoming stock may include multiple records for a specific product / variant and as such the ETA and Quantity for each order expected is included in the results of each endpoint.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these requirements
    1. Throughout the day
        1. NOTE: only once the Full Get Stock end point has called
            
        2. This is because this end point is only the rolling changes since the 1st call of the day (00:30)
            
    2. Once you have the rolling information you can use this to update your master stock as this will only include the changes since the initial daily sync
        
    3. This end point includes a last updated date time stamp so you can use this to filter the results within your application to only action the items since your last Get Updated call
        

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Called once a day to get the stock in sync
    
    1. This endpoint only returns the changes from the 1st daily call
        
    2. Therefore, if you call it once a day you will get only those changed since then and you will NOT receive the full product stock list
        

**IMPORTANT:**

Stock provided by these endpoints match on the product variant's **Full Code**. We do provide Stock for the Colour Groupings and Totals for the entire product set. This is outlined in the "Stock" introduction above.

item.description: This module is used to get Stock specific information. This includes the following 3 stock types: 1. Total for a Base Product 1. Sum of all variants across any colour and any size 2. Type = 0 2. Total per colour 1. Sum of all variants grouped by colour 2. Type = 1 3. Total per variant 1. Stock for a variant of a specific colour and size 2. Type = 2 **IMPORTANT:** Only use the "Total per variant" (Type = 2) for managing stock levels. The other types are purely for visual usages and should not be used for availability and or ordering processes. These endpoints return the current and incoming stock levels per product and includes: 1. Product Code 2. Stock Type (outlined above) 3. Current Stock Levels 4. Incoming Stock 1. Amount 2. ETA ## How to match the Stock to the correct Product Stock provided by these endpoints match on the product variant's **Full Code**. For Stock there is matching on the Base and Colour grouping as outlined above. # Frequency - **Get All** - This is updated multiple times a day (2-3) - **Get Updated** / Rolling Stock - Is updated every few minutes throughout the day - Only contains the rolling stock updates for that day - It is cleared each day at the same time as the 1st get all updates Because the updated stock is updated regularly it will continue to increase in record size, however we include a modified date therefore you are able to get all updated records and then filter on your side only the ones required. **NOTE:** In the updated endpoint we only include the latest stock for a product. This means if it has changed 10 times the endpoint will only include the last / latest value. # Recommend Implementation The purpose of these endpoints is to provide the correct information to each customer to save and manage on their own system. This will help streamline ordering systems and websites as your stock would be accurate and won't require additional product only calls. ## Stay in Sync throughout the day Each implementation and requirements from each customer is different however we have outlined a general recommend best practice implementation guide / process: - Get All once a day - Update all your local stock records as per these results - Get Updates every 10-15 mins or when needed - Use this data to update your local stock values - Only implemented **after** the Get All has been run - If no changes have been made this would return 0 (zero) records - This would mean the get all is the latest stock value ## Check stock before placing an order If you need to check stock of a few products before placing an order there would be 2 scenarios: 1. You ran a full sync in the morning and have been running the rolling updates throughout the day 1. In this instance your stock may be only slightly out since the last call therefore you could call the Stock Update endpoint 1. Search through the results for the products you require 1. If the products are in the updated results then that value in the update endpoint is the latest 2. If they are not in the updated results it means the values you have are the latest 2. You have not kept stock in sync 1. 1st call the full endpoint 2. 2nd call the updated endpoint 3. Check the updated endpoint 1. If those products are found, meaning it has been updated since full endpoint, then use the updated endpoint value 2. if those products are not found, meaning it has not been updated since full endpoint, therefore use the full endpoint value ## Access You are required to have a valid token as well as authorization to call the endpoints in this module.

item.name: Prices

request.description:
The purpose of this endpoint is to return the latest prices for all products within a specific time frame.

This endpoint returns **only Product** pricing, all branding and inclusive branding related pricing is found in other endpoints.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these requirements
    
    1. Once a day in order to get all prices in sync
        
    2. Once a week if you are using the Updated endpoint daily
        
    3. If Amrod annouced a bulk price increase date this should be called on that day to ensure all prices are up to date
        

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Called multiple times within the day
    
    1. This will result in sending all product pricing even when nothing has since changed
        

**IMPORTANT:**

Prices provided by these endpoints match to the product variant's **Full Code**. There is no matching of prices to the **Simple Code** of a product.

The purpose of this endpoint is to return only the updated or changed prices since the previous day.

EndFragment

It should be used in conjunction with the Get All endpoint that should be called first and then this endpoint can be called once a day.

This endpoint returns **only Product** pricing, all branding and inclusive branding related pricing is found in other endpoints.

An "Action" field is returned for each record and is the only field returned on these endpoints.

The field is called "ActionType" and it denotes the type of change. It is an INT field that correlates to the following types / status / actions:

- **0**
    
    - This means it has been created
        
- **1**
    
    - This means that record has changed or been updated
        
- **2**
    
    - This means this record has been removed
        

It is provided to advise you on what changes they include and what actions to implement.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these requirements
    
    1. Once a day in order to get all prices in sync
        
    2. This should be called only after the full Get Prices endpoint is called to get the initial data imported
        

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Called multiple times within the day
    
    1. This will result in sending all product prices changed for that day
        
    2. They do not change within a day
        
    3. If Amrod announced a bulk price increase date this endpoint should not be used and we rather recommend using the Get All to ensure all prices are up to date
        

**IMPORTANT:**

Prices provided by these endpoints match to the product variant's **Full Code**. There is no matching of prices to the **Simple Code** of a product.

item.description:
This module is used to get all or specific Product Prices. This includes the product codes as well as the price.

To get the pricing for branding methods please use the "Branding Prices" endpoints.

The price is based on your users Tier and within your regions currency.

## How to match the Price to the correct Product

Prices provided by these endpoints match to the product variant's **Full Code**. There is no matching on prices to the **Simple Code** of a product.

## Update Frequency

Amrod updates Product _Pricing_ only once a day.

## Access

You are required to have a valid token as well as authorization to call the endpoints in this module.

Should you require assistance with access please contact your Account Manager.

## VAT

All prices are provided EXCLUDING VAT. If you are adding VAT we recommend using these rules based on how Amrod processes VAT:

- Round Price to 2 decimal places
    
- Add VAT
    
- Round again to 2 decimal places
    
- When multiplying quantities to get a final amount
    
    - Multiply quantity with the final rounded amount and then round 1 last time

item.name: Categories

request.description:
The purpose of this endpoint is to return **All** the Categories.

It returns a full tree structure with nested categories.

The purpose of this endpoint is if you wish to preload all the lookup categories. One could then use the Id of the category found in the **Products** endpoint to know which category to assign the product too.

A category image has been included should you wish to use that in your implementation.

**NOTE:**  
Categories do not change often therefore it may not need to be called so frequently. If you have already loaded the full category tree / structure we would recommend using the **Get Updated** endpoint to check if anything has changed since the last import / sync.

The purpose of this endpoint is to return **All** the Categories.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these 3 requirements
    1. Daily
        1. This information can be called daily if required to keep the information as up to date as possible
            
    2. Weekly
        1. The Categories do not get updated often therefore they can be called either weekly or monthly to keep the information in sync and ensure you have the latest information
            
    3. On Demand
        1. The Categories do not get updated often therefore they can be called only when new Categories are noticed in the Product structure
            

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Missing a day
    1. The Get Updated endpoint only shows changes in last 24hrs
        
    2. If a day was missed you may have missed information therefore the data may be out of sync
        
    3. If this occurs, we recommend running the Get All endpoint to make sure you have the latest information
        
2. Called multiple times within the day
    1. As above, items are not changed within a day, therefore it is not needed to be called multiple times within a day

The purpose of this endpoint is to return only the **Updated** Categories.

An "Action" field is returned for each record only returned on these endpoints.

The field is called "ActionType" and it denotes the type of change. It is an INT field that correlates to the following types / status / actions:

- **0**
    - This means it has been created
- **1**
    - This means that record has changed or been updated
- **2**
    - This means this record has been removed

It is provided to advise you on what changes they include and what actions to implement.

This **Get Updated** endpoint returns only the information changed since the previous day. If you miss a day or would like to get all information in order to keep all info in sync please use the **All** endpoint.

**NOTE:**  
If any item is changed within a node structure the entire tree for all those items related are returned and not just the node that was changed.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is called Daily for these 2 requirements
    1. This is based on the assumption that the Get All endpoint has been called and information saved locally
    2. After this endpoint has been run, then the Get Changed is run daily to check if any changes have been made

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Missing a day
    1. The Get Updated endpoint only shows changes from the previous day
    2. If a day was missed you may have missed information therefore the data may be out of sync
    3. If this occurs, we recommend running the Get All endpoint to make sure you have the latest information
2. Called multiple times within the day
    1. As above, items are not changed within a day, therefore it is not needed to be called multiple times within a day

item.description:
This module is used to get the Inclusive Branding information. This includes all the information related to Amrod's inclusive offerings. ## Branding Position This endpoint includes a Branding Position parameter. This is required because if product or giftset has multiple components there maybe situations whereby the same method can be done on multiple items. In this scenario you would need to utilise the position field to make sure you are applying the inclusive branding special to the correct position. This field contains a string of Positions Codes split by a ^ separator. You will need to use the flags and other field rules to determine if this is exclusive, all positions, 1st position, etc. ## Return Structure and Rules The information returned is as per the current legacy xml feed and includes the following fields:
Variable Name
Type
Required / Optional
Purpose
InclusiveBrandingNa
me
String
Required
A display name to uniquely identify the Inclusive Branding
InclusiveBrandingFlags
Long
Required
This Flag outlines how the special can be applied. There are the following flags:

None = 0,
Disabled = 1,
OnlyApplicableProducts = 2,
MutualExclusiveBrandingSpecial = 4

A special can be either for all products (0), or for specific products (2), but currently all specials are for applicable products only.
Specials cannot be deleted; they are just disabled (1), and then won’t apply to any products.

Some specials are set to be mutually exclusive (4).
This means this special cannot be used together with any other special. The product can still be linked to other specials, but on the calculator or online ordering you will not be allowed to select more than one special if one of your choices is mutually exclusive.

An example would be BAS-7776 that is set up with both “Inclusive of 1Position Embroidery1” and “Inclusive Of 1Position Digital Transfer1”.
The embroidery special has the mutually exclusive flag, so if you select it on position A-1, and then select digital transfer on position B, then the following pop-up message will display: “Please note selecting the special removed all other mutually exclusive specials where the specials currently applied.”

If doing it the other way around where digital transfer is selected on position B, and the user then selects embroidery on position A-1, then the following message is displayed: “Please note selecting the mutually exclusive special removed all other specials where the specials currently applied.”

Client-side validation is in place on the interface to prevent the user from selecting both specials and will render a message accordingly.
BrandingPositionType
Int
Required
The Type of position this Inclusive Branding applies to. A Position type can be seen as a rule to specify that only 1 position is
inclusive or the only the first position is inclusive but any additional positions are offered at a discounted price. The
possible values for position types are:

public enum ProductInclusive BrandingBrandigPositionType
{
//
// Summary:
// Any Position following the first initial inclusive branding position
AndThereafter = -1,
//
// Summary:
// Any one Position, but only one
AnyOnePosition = -2,
//
// Summary:
// All Positions, no limit of the number of positions applicable to
AnyPosition = -3,
//
// Summary:
// The first Position Only. Position A / A1,A2 ...
AnyPosition = 1,
//
// Summary:
// The second Position Only. Position B
AnyPosition = 2,
//
// Summary:
// The third Position Only. Position C
AnyPosition = 3,
//
// Summary:
// The fourth Position Only. Position D
AnyPosition = 4,
//
// Summary:
// The fith Position Only. Position E
AnyPosition = 5,
//
// Summary:
// The six Position Only. Position F
AnyPosition = 6
}
BrandingPositions
String
Required
The positions for this product where the inclusive branding special is placed on.

It returns a string of position codes separated by a ^ character.

In an example where the string value is "A^B" it means this special applies to the product with the specific method only on positions A and B.
The BrandingPositionType will determine / guide you as to if it is either each position, 1st position, mutually exclusive, etc.
BrandingMethod
String
Required
The branding method that this Inclusive Branding applies to
BrandingReplacementCode
String
Optional
The replacement code that will be invoiced when this Inclusive Branding applies. SA-1-A will normally be replaced by SA-FREE
when an Inclusive Branding is applied
NumberOfC
olours
numeric
Required
The number of Branding colours that this Inclusive Branding is applicable for. Most Inclusive Brandings are only applicable to 1 colour
branding
MinQuantity
numeric
Required
The minimum number of products required to make this Inclusive Branding applicable
BrandingFl
ags
Long
Optional
Specifies addition detail on the behaviour of a Inclusive Branding: Possible Values are

[Flags]
public enum ProductInclusiveBrandingBrandigDetailFlags
{
//
// Summary:
// No Addition info
None = 0,
//
// Summary:
// The price will always be 0 for this branding special, even if the
price field has a value
ZeroPricing = 1,
//
// Summary:
// The price will be calculated from the configured Tier price per
Amrod's Accounting system. This value is provided as "BrandingPrice" field
UsePastelPricing = 2
}
ValidFrom
Date
Required
The date from which this Inclusive Branding is valid from
ValidTo
Date
Required
The date to which this Inclusive Branding is valid to. Any Inclusive Brandings that have passed the ValidTo date will no longer be included
in the Feed and should be removed
BrandingCo
de
String
Optional
The code of the branding method that can be used with this Inclusive Branding
SimpleCode
String
Required
The product SimpleCode that for which this Inclusive Branding applies to.
Colour
String
Optional
The product segment colour for which this Inclusive Branding applies to. Most Inclusive Brandings apply to All colour variants, but with the
exception there will be that are only applicable to select colour variants.
## Update Frequency Amrod updates Inclusive Branding information only once a day. ## Access You are required to have a valid token as well as authorization to call the endpoints in this module.

item.name: Branding Prices

request.description:
The purpose of this endpoint is to return **All** the Branding Prices.

The purpose of this endpoint is to return the list of all branding prices to be used as lookups when calculating prices / quotes based on specific branding methods.

These endpoints return the unique branding codes as well as the different price and quantity breaks (e.g. 1-99 Price X, 100-199 Price Y).

This endpoint includes the list of items based on the number of colours and the setup and unit cost per price break and per number of colours.

This would be used should you wish to offer a full branding service whereby you can calculate the cost of the branding based on quantity, position and method.

**NOTE:**  
Branding Prices do not change often therefore it may not need to be called so frequently. If you have already loaded the full list we would recommend using the **Get Updated** endpoint to check if anything has changed since the last import / sync.

The purpose of this endpoint is to return **All** the Branding Prices.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these 3 requirements
    1. Daily
        1. This information can be called daily if required to keep the information as up to date as possible
    2. Weekly
        1. The Branding Prices do not get updated often therefore they can be called either weekly or monthly to keep the information in sync and ensure you have the latest information
    3. On Demand
        1. The Branding Prices do not get updated often therefore they can be called only when new brands are noticed in the Product structure

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. This endpoint is not required if you do not offer branding in your order flow or website
2. Missing a day
    1. The Get Updated endpoint only shows changes in last 24hrs
    2. If a day was missed you may have missed information therefore the data may be out of sync
    3. If this occurs, we recommend running the Get All endpoint to make sure you have the latest information
3. Called multiple times within the day
    1. As above, items are not changed within a day, therefore it is not needed to be called multiple times within a day

1. It is recommended it is called Daily for these 2 requirements
    1. This is based on the assumption that the Get All endpoint has been called and information saved locally
    2. After this endpoint has been run, then the Get Changed is run daily to check if any changes have been made

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. This endpoint is not required if you do not offer branding in your order flow or website
2. Missing a day
    1. The Get Updated endpoint only shows changes from the previous day
    2. If a day was missed you may have missed information therefore the data may be out of sync
    3. If this occurs, we recommend running the Get All endpoint to make sure you have the latest information
3. Called multiple times within the day
    1. As above, items are not changed within a day, therefore it is not needed to be called multiple times within a day

item.description:
This module is used to get all Branding Prices per Branding Code.

To get the product pricing please use the "Prices" endpoints.

Branding Prices provide the data returned based on Branding Code and should be used as a lookup table to dynamically match the appropriate branding code required per product quantity, colours, etc as outlined below.

This endpoint returns branding method, print codes, quantity breaks (e.g. 1-99 Price X, 100-199 Price Y) as well as the setup and unit cost per price break.

It includes the Number of Colours which is important as some products may include multiple branding positions using the same branding method and therefore it is important to use the correct combination of colour, methods, and quantity.

This would be used should you wish to offer a full branding service whereby you can calculate the cost of the branding based on quantity, position and method.

The reason why this has been split out and not included in Product Pricing or Product Information is that it is specific information related to when you are offering branding as a solution / service and you need the price to calculate an amount to add to cart or within a back office ordering and quoting tool.

The information returned includes Branding Method Codes, Print Code, Quantity Breaks, Setup and Branding Prices. You would then be able to use this information to map product to their branding position, method, branding code and number of colours to this branding price lookup table.

The price is based on your users Tier.

This endpoint includes the Inclusive Branding Special FREE codes and pricing. To determine the price / mapping to the right price please use the **ReplacementCode** found in the Branding Specials endpoint and lookup this code's pricing within the "Branding Price" endpoint

## Update Frequency

Amrod updates Product Branding _Pricing_ only once a day.

## Access

You are required to have a valid token as well as authorization to call the endpoints in this module.

## VAT

All prices are provided EXCLUDING VAT. If you are adding VAT we recommend using these rules based on the how the financial system works:

- Round Price to 2 decimal places
- Add VAT
- Round again to 2 decimal places
- When multiplying quantities to get a final amount
    - Multiply quantity with the final rounded amount and then round 1 last time

This folder is used to group all Branding related modules and endpoints together. This includes Branding Prices and Branding Special information and Pricing

## Update Frequency

Amrod updates _All Branding_ information only once a day.

## Access

You are required to have a valid token as well as authorization to call the endpoints in this module.

item.name: Colour Swatches

request.description:
The purpose of this endpoint is to return the list of all our different product colours and their HEX value.

**NOTE:**  
This endpoint has been updated to <u>remove </u> the IsDeleted field / flag as it been deprecated.

We have expanded our colour swatches to better reflect the colour and as such have also provided a **Grouped Colour Swatch** endpoint which can be used to group similar colours if needed.

It is important to remember that some colours may not appear in a group e.g. Black is a single colour and doesn't have a grouping therefore one would always need to call this **Get Colour Swatches** endpoint to get all the colours first and then use the grouping if they wish to group certain colours.

This endpoint returns **all colours** and doesn't have "get updated" as the colours don't change often.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these requirements
    
    1. If you wish to display colour swatches on your website
        
    2. If you wish to align your colours with the same colour HEX values used on Amrod's website
        
    3. It changes very infrequently so it can be called once a month OR when you notice a new colour is loaded and you don't have a colour for it.
        

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Called multiple times within the day or multiple times a week
    
    1. The information changes very infrequently therefore there is no need to call it multiple times within a week
        
2. We recommend it is called manually and as and when needed instead of scheduled to be called often

The purpose of this endpoint is to return the list of all our different product colour groupings and their related colours within each group.

Each group returns an array of colour codes based on the colours within that group. These codes are mapped to the codes in the **Get Colour Swatches** endpoint.

**NOTE:**  
It is important to remember that some colours may not appear in a group e.g. Black is a single colour and doesn't have a grouping therefore one would always need to call the **Get Colour Swatches** endpoint to get all the colours first and then use the grouping if they wish to group certain colours.

This endpoint returns **all colour groupings** and doesn't have "get updated" as the colours don't change often.

## When this _should_ be used

1. It is recommended it is only called for these requirements
    
    1. If you wish to **display** colour swatches on your website
        
    2. If you wish to **group** colour swatches on your website
        
    3. If you wish to align your colours with the same colour HEX values used on Amrod's website
        
    4. It changes very infrequently so it can be called once a month OR when you notice a new colour is loaded and you don't have a colour for it.
        

## When this _should not_ be used

This endpoint should not be used in the following scenarios

1. Called multiple times within the day or multiple times a week
    
    1. The information changes very infrequently therefore there is no need to call it multiple times within a week
        
2. We recommend it is called manually and as and when needed instead of scheduled to be called often

item.description:
This module is used to get a list of the colour swatches and their HEX values.

This can be used if you would like to show the correct colour swatch or block or use within your website or system.

## Colour Grouping

As we add additional colour swatches we have extended the feature to also included a Colour Grouping endpoint. The purpose of this is to provide a grouping of similar colours e.g. A group called "Blue" would include Cyan, Light Blue and Dark Blue.

**Note:** It is important to note that some colours may not appear in a group e.g. Black is a single colour and doesn't have a grouping therefore one would always need to call the **Get Colour Swatches** endpoint to get all the colours first and then use the grouping if they wish to group certain colours.

## Update Frequency

As our colour swatches don't change often this is only updated when we launch a new colour.

## Access

You are required to have a valid token as well as authorization to call the endpoints in this module.

Should you require assistance with access please contact your Account Manager.

## Name

We provide a friendly name of the colour for you to use if you require and need a descriptor for the colour.

## Code

Each colour has a code. This is unique and used to define only 1 specific colour.

## HEX

All colours include their HEX value. This may include a single value or an array of comma separate # values. This would, for example, when there are 2 colours such as Camouflage ("#8C9458","#D9D189","#4C5B44","#1E2E21") or if the item has a triwm colour such as Black Cyan ("#000000","#00B2E2").

This documentation outlines the Customer /  
Vendor / API integration endpoints, how to use them and recommendations and suggestions.

## API Available Times - 00:00 -> 01:00 (GTM+2)

The Vendor API availability has been updated and will no longer be available between 00:00 and 01:00 (GMT+2). This is to allow our internal systems to sync, manipulate, prepare and ensure all information is up to date and available for our customers.

Any calls to this API during this time will return a 204 HTTP response. This is a successful call and response however will not return any data.

# Audience

## Amrod Clients and Vendors

This documentation highlights and explains how to gather information for Amrod's products, categories, pricing and stock to be used for either clients' websites or backend systems.

## Developers

This documentation is aimed at technical developers and integrators that are implementing an "integration layer" using Amrod's Product Data with their clients' website or internal systems.

## Important URLS

**Authentication URL**

{{IdentityServerUrl}}

**Vendor API URL**

{{CatalogueVendorApiUrl}}

**Get Access Token URL**

{{CatalogueVendorApiUrl}}/VendorLogin

## "The API is not working in my browser"

**You cannot access the vendor API by visiting the url in a web browser**  
The API does not allow you to access the information by visiting it in a browser. It needs to be connected via a IDE, backend code or postman. Please see [https://forum.amrod.co.za/topic/37/can-i-get-a-token-by-visiting-the-url-in-my-browser](https://forum.amrod.co.za/topic/37/can-i-get-a-token-by-visiting-the-url-in-my-browser) for more info.

# Usage

These endpoints are provided to allow users to quickly and efficiently gather the correct information required and optimize your own integration to offer a seamless, quick and best practice solution to your needs.

## Endpoint Structure

All modules have 2 main endpoints

1. Get All
    
    1. This returns the list of ALL items
        
2. Get Changes
    
    1. This returns only the changes made from the previous day
        

## Get All

This endpoint returns **ALL** the information from a specific module / area.

## Get Changes

This endpoint returns only the records that have **changed** since the previous day.

An Action field is returned for each record and is only returned on these endpoints.

The field is called "ActionType" and it denotes the type of change. It is an INT field that correlates to the following types / status / actions:

- **0**
    
    - This means it has been created
        
- **1**
    
    - This means that record has changed or been updated
        
- **2**
    
    - This means this record has been removed
        

It is provided to advise you on what changes are included and what actions to implement.

# Recommended Implementation

Each implementation and the requirements from each customer is different, however we have outlined a general recommended implementation guide / process:

- Get All once a day or once a week
    
- Get Changes once a day going forward after Get All was run initially.
    
    - If no changes have been made this would return 0 (zero) records
        

# Frequency

Except for Stock, information is only updated once a day therefore if you have requested and processed the information for that day there is no need to request it again. The information you would receive on a 2nd call (within the same day) would not be different and it would result in processing the same information you already have.
